definition: Error from overly simplistic assumptions in a learning algorithm. High bias causes underfitting — the model fails to capture patterns in the training data.
Error from excessive sensitivity to small fluctuations in the training set. High variance causes overfitting — the model memorizes noise instead of learning general patterns.
Regularization technique that randomly sets a fraction of neural network neurons to zero during training, preventing co-adaptation and overfitting.
TensorFlow decorator that converts Python functions to TensorFlow graphs, enabling faster execution by avoiding Python overhead and enabling optimizations.
TensorFlow's binary storage format that bundles serialized tf.train.Example protos. Enables efficient shuffling, compression, and parallel reads for large datasets.
Google Cloud service for custom text classification, entity extraction, and sentiment analysis with labeled data. Lowest-effort option for custom NLP tasks.
